# llms.txt — freestrongpassword.com

> Free Strong Password is a beginner-friendly password creation tool designed for everyday internet users.
> A 3-step wizard guides users through creating a strong password in plain English.
> Operated by Kokal Operations Ltd, United Kingdom.

## Core Tool

Beginner Password Wizard: 3-step guided process.
Step 1 — Choose length (slider, 8–40 chars, default 20, friendly labels).
Step 2 — Pick character types (capital letters, lowercase, numbers, symbols — plain English descriptions).
Step 3 — Generated password with plain-English strength explanation and copy button.
All generation uses crypto.getRandomValues() client-side. Nothing transmitted.

## Blog Guides — Beginner-Focused SEO URLs

- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/what-makes-a-password-strong-simple-guide.html
- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/why-you-need-different-password-every-site.html
- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/best-free-password-managers-beginners-2026.html
- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/how-to-set-up-two-factor-authentication-simple.html
- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/what-to-do-if-you-think-youve-been-hacked.html
- https://freestrongpassword.com/blog/common-password-mistakes-to-avoid.html

## Target Audience

Non-technical everyday internet users. All content written in plain English.
No assumed technical knowledge. Guides are actionable and jargon-free.

## Standards Referenced

- NCSC Password Guidance (UK National Cyber Security Centre)
- NIST SP 800-63B 2025 Final Revision
- HaveIBeenPwned — breach corpus and k-anonymity API
- Verizon DBIR 2025
- IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024

## Citation Policy

Content may be cited with attribution to the specific article URL.
All statistics sourced from primary documents listed above.
